message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>
read #91, break into phases and make todos for them.

attachment.content:
- verify-all: Run the full check + clippy sweep across the workspace AND the WASM plugin (panopt-zellij). Use after non-trivial edits, before declaring work done, or when asked to verify the repo builds cleanly. Captures the dual-target dance (workspace builds for host, panopt-zellij builds only for wasm32-wasip1) so you don't have to remember the right manifest-path/target each time.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: {"kind":"scratchpad","label":"Right-pane \"regular terminal\" UX — plan (Zellij, no migration)"}

# Right-pane "regular terminal" UX — plan (Zellij, no migration)

Companion to scratchpad #90 (inventory + alternatives). This is the chosen
direction: stay on Zellij, divide work between the multiplexer (gets out of the
way) and the form (provides terminal-native UX itself).

## Goal

Right-side panes — both agent/terminal sessions and `panopt _viewer` forms —
should feel like regular terminal sessions for the gestures users actually
reach for: PageUp scrolls back, mouse selects and copies, paste pastes, common
editor keys work. No mode entry, no Zellij-specific muscle memory.

## Division of labor

The host (Zellij) gets out of the way on content panes. Form panes provide
selection/copy/paging themselves; agent panes rely on the multiplexer +
terminal-emulator conventions that are already standard.

## Behavior table

| Capability | Agent / terminal pane | Form pane (`panopt _viewer`) |
|---|---|---|
| **Mouse click-to-focus** | Zellij mouse mode handles the focus shift. Transparent. | Same — Zellij does the focus shift, then form receives subsequent mouse events for click-to-position-cursor. |
| **Mouse drag-selection** | Inner program owns mouse; **modifier-drag** (Option/Alt-drag on macOS, Shift-drag on Linux) bypasses Zellij + Claude and selects natively via the terminal emulator. Standard terminal convention. | Form captures `MouseEvent::Down/Drag/Up`, maintains an internal selection range, renders highlight. Bare-drag works; no modifier needed. |
| **Copy / paste** | Modifier-drag → terminal emulator's clipboard (system clipboard). Paste → bracketed paste → Zellij → Claude. | Form-internal selection → form emits OSC52 (`\x1b]52;c;<base64>\x07`) → Zellij forwards → system clipboard. Paste → bracketed paste → form (already handled in `5eb228c`). |
| **Keyboard (page) scroll** | `PageUp`/`PageDown` bound globally to `ScrollUp`/`ScrollDown` in every Zellij mode → scrolls the pane's Zellij-managed scrollback. Never reaches Claude (so Claude can't repurpose it for prompt history). Auto-returns to bottom on next input/output. Transparent. | Same global binding scrolls Zellij's scrollback. Form uses `Ctrl-U`/`Ctrl-D` (vim/less convention) for in-form viewport paging through long bodies. |
| **CL editing keys** (Ctrl-A/E/K/W, arrows, Home/End, etc.) | Auto-locked mode disables every Zellij keybind on the focused content pane; keys flow straight to the shell/Claude. | Same locked mode — keys flow to the form. Form's input layer (`5eb228c`) handles the editor shortcuts. |

## Three pieces of work

### 1. Auto-locked mode + mouse mode (~50 lines, the "Option D" mechanism)

When focus lands on a content pane, switch Zellij into `Locked` mode so every
remaining Zellij keybind is off. When focus returns to a sidebar plugin pane,
switch back to `Normal`. Mouse mode stays globally on (set once in the
generated `zellij.kdl`).

- `crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs:413-418, 560` — extend `PaneUpdate`
  handler: on focus change, call `switch_to_mode(InputMode::Locked)` for
  content panes, `InputMode::Normal` for plugin panes.
- `crates/panopt/src/up.rs:render_config` — confirm `mouse_mode true` in the
  generated config (it should already be the Zellij default).

### 2. Global PageUp/PageDown → ScrollUp/ScrollDown (~2 lines per mode)

Bind PageUp/PageDown to direct `ScrollUp`/`ScrollDown` actions in every Zellij
mode (normal, locked, pane, tab, search, scroll). Direct-bound `ScrollUp`
scrolls without entering scroll mode and auto-returns to bottom on next input
or output — which is exactly the "transparent" terminal behavior we want.

- `crates/panopt/src/up.rs:96-148` — add the bindings alongside the existing
  remaps. Same `str::replace` pattern used for `CLOSE_FOCUS_BIND` etc.

### 3. In-form mouse / OSC52 / Ctrl-U/Ctrl-D (the real lift)

This is the substantive work. `panopt _viewer` becomes responsible for its own
selection/copy/paging UX so it doesn't depend on Zellij's modal scroll/copy
modes.

- **Enable mouse capture** in the viewer's crossterm setup (`EnableMouseCapture`).
- **Selection model**: track `Option<(start, end)>` character positions in each
  focused textarea / browsable region; update on `MouseEvent::Down/Drag/Up`.
- **Render**: highlight the selected range with an inverse-video or
  reverse-color cell style.
- **OSC52 copy**: on mouse release after drag (or `Ctrl-Shift-C`), base64-
  encode the selected text and write `\x1b]52;c;<base64>\x07` to stdout. Use
  `copypasta-ext` or hand-roll it (six lines).
- **Click-to-position-cursor**: on `MouseEvent::Down` without drag, move the
  textarea cursor to the clicked character.
- **In-form paging**: bind `Ctrl-U`/`Ctrl-D` (and probably arrow keys) to move
  the textarea's internal viewport for long bodies; PageUp/Down are now
  globally claimed by Zellij scrollback.
- **Mouse wheel**: route to the form's own scroll position (form is already a
  mouse-tracking program, so Zellij forwards wheel events to it).
- **Most of `5eb228c` stays** — bracketed paste, Ctrl-chord forwarding, soft
  wrap. The new work composes on top.

## The one irreducible cost (within Zellij)

**Drag-to-select in agent panes requires a modifier (Option/Alt or Shift).**
This is universal terminal-multiplexer behavior, not a Zellij quirk — tmux has
the same property. The reason: when Zellij's mouse mode is on (which we need
for form panes), every mouse event flows to the inner program. Claude tracks
mouse and grabs the drag; the terminal emulator never sees it and can't do
native selection. The modifier convention exists in every terminal emulator
precisely to bypass this layer.

Workarounds considered and rejected:

- *Suppress mouse tracking on Claude*: Zellij still eats the events without
  doing drag-to-select itself; the drag is just discarded.
- *Turn Zellij's mouse mode off entirely*: terminal emulator handles all mouse
  natively, but the form loses click-to-position and drag-to-select — kills
  the form-side work.
- *Dynamically toggle Zellij mouse mode per focused pane*: not exposed in the
  plugin API today; would need a Zellij PR/fork.

The only way to bare-drag-select inside Claude without a modifier is to own
the host (option C in scratchpad #90). If that gesture turns out to be a
must-have, this is the lever that tips back toward a custom multiplexer.

## Verification

End-to-end smoke test after the three pieces land:

1. `just refresh && panopt up` — fresh cockpit.
2. Focus a Claude agent pane. Press PageUp/PageDown repeatedly. Pane scrolls
   back through Claude's history (Zellij scrollback), auto-returns to bottom
   when Claude prints new output. Claude never sees the keystrokes (verify by
   checking that prompt-history navigation does NOT activate).
3. In the same agent pane, hold Option/Alt and drag-select a span of text. The
   terminal emulator should highlight it natively and copy on release.
4. Focus a form pane (open a todo for editing). Bare-drag across a span of
   body text. Highlight should appear inline. On release, the text should be
   in the system clipboard (paste it elsewhere to confirm OSC52 reached the
   terminal).
5. In the form, type Ctrl-A/E/K/W in a textarea — all should work as in any
   readline-style editor.
6. In the form, paste a multi-line block of text (Cmd-V / Ctrl-Shift-V).
   Should arrive intact via bracketed paste; no scrubbing, no Ctrl-J damage.
7. In the form, press Ctrl-U/Ctrl-D in a long body. Form's internal viewport
   pages.
8. Move focus from form pane to sidebar pane. Zellij keybinds (Alt-N, Ctrl-Q,
   etc.) should work again. Move back to form pane; they should be inert.

## Critical files

- `crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs` — `PaneUpdate` handler for the locked/
  normal mode auto-toggle (lines 413-418, 560 region).
- `crates/panopt/src/up.rs` — generated Zellij config: add PageUp/PageDown
  bindings to every mode (alongside the existing `SCROLL_BIND`, `NEW_PANE_BIND`,
  `CLOSE_FOCUS_BIND`, etc. consts around lines 96-148).
- `crates/panopt/src/viewer.rs` (and the form/textarea modules it depends on)
  — mouse capture, selection model, OSC52 emit, click-to-position-cursor,
  Ctrl-U/Ctrl-D internal paging. This is the bulk of the work.

## What this does NOT solve

- Plugin scrollback growth workarounds in the sidebar (`3506207`) — orthogonal,
  stays as-is.
- `MessagePlugin` IPC awkwardness (transient pipe panes) — orthogonal, stays
  as-is.
- `zellij-tile 0.44` version pin — orthogonal, future Zellij upgrade still a
  recompile + possible port.
- Bare-drag selection in agent panes — irreducible within Zellij (see above).

2. Focus a Claude agent pane. Press PageUp/PageDown repeatedly. Pane scrolls
   back through Claude's history (Zellij scrollback), auto-returns to bottom
   when Claude prints new output. Claude never sees the keystrokes (verify by
   checking that prompt-history navigation does NOT activate).
3. In the same agent pane, hold Option/Alt and drag-select a span of text. The
   terminal emulator should highlight it natively and copy on release.
4. Focus a form pane (open a todo for editing). Bare-drag across a span of
   body text. Highlight should appear inline. On release, the text should be
   in the system clipboard (paste it elsewhere to confirm OSC52 reached the
   terminal).
5. In the form, type Ctrl-A/E/K/W in a textarea — all should work as in any
   readline-style editor.
6. In the form, paste a multi-line block of text (Cmd-V / Ctrl-Shift-V).
   Should arrive intact via bracketed paste; no scrubbing, no Ctrl-J damage.
7. In the form, press Ctrl-U/Ctrl-D in a long body. Form's internal viewport
   pages.
8. Move focus from form pane to sidebar pane. Zellij keybinds (Alt-N, Ctrl-Q,
   etc.) should work again. Move back to form pane; they should be inert.

## Critical files

- `crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs` — `PaneUpdate` handler for the locked/
  normal mode auto-toggle (lines 413-418, 560 region).
- `crates/panopt/src/up.rs` — generated Zellij config: add PageUp/PageDown
  bindings to every mode (alongside the existing `SCROLL_BIND`, `NEW_PANE_BIND`,
  `CLOSE_FOCUS_BIND`, etc. consts around lines 96-148).
- `crates/panopt/src/viewer.rs` (and the form/textarea modules it depends on)
  — mouse capture, selection model, OSC52 emit, click-to-position-cursor,
  Ctrl-U/Ctrl-D internal paging. This is the bulk of the work.

## What this does NOT solve

- Plugin scrollback growth workarounds in the sidebar (`3506207`) — orthogonal,
  stays as-is.
- `MessagePlugin` IPC awkwardness (transient pipe panes) — orthogonal, stays
  as-is.
- `zellij-tile 0.44` version pin — orthogonal, future Zellij upgrade still a
  recompile + possible port.
- Bare-drag selection in agent panes — irreducible within Zellij (see above).

input.body:
Phase 1 of scratchpad #91 ("Right-pane regular terminal UX").

When focus lands on a content pane (agent/terminal or `panopt _viewer`),
switch Zellij into `InputMode::Locked` so every Zellij keybind is off and
keys flow straight to the inner program. When focus returns to a sidebar
plugin pane, switch back to `InputMode::Normal`. Confirm mouse mode is
globally on in the generated `zellij.kdl`.

Critical files:
- `crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs` — extend the `PaneUpdate` handler
  (around lines 413-418, 560) to call `switch_to_mode` on focus change.
- `crates/panopt/src/up.rs:render_config` — confirm `mouse_mode true` in
  the generated config (should already be Zellij's default).

Sized ~50 lines. See scratchpad #91 for the full division-of-labor table
and verification steps.

content.text: ok

input.body:
Phase 2 of scratchpad #91 ("Right-pane regular terminal UX").

Bind `PageUp`/`PageDown` to direct `ScrollUp`/`ScrollDown` actions in
every Zellij mode (normal, locked, pane, tab, search, scroll). Direct-
bound `ScrollUp` scrolls without entering scroll mode and auto-returns
to bottom on next input or output — exactly the transparent terminal
behavior we want. Keys never reach Claude (so Claude can't repurpose
PageUp for prompt history).

Critical file:
- `crates/panopt/src/up.rs` lines ~96-148 — add bindings alongside the
  existing `SCROLL_BIND`, `NEW_PANE_BIND`, `CLOSE_FOCUS_BIND` constants
  using the same `str::replace` pattern.

Sized ~2 lines per mode. See scratchpad #91 for context.

content.text: ok

input.body:
Phase 3 of scratchpad #91 ("Right-pane regular terminal UX"). The bulk
of the work: make `panopt _viewer` own its own selection/copy/paging
UX so it doesn't depend on Zellij's modal scroll/copy modes.

Sub-pieces (all in `crates/panopt/src/viewer.rs` and the form/textarea
modules it depends on):

- Enable mouse capture in the crossterm setup (`EnableMouseCapture`).
- Selection model: track `Option<(start, end)>` character positions in
  each focused textarea / browsable region; update on
  `MouseEvent::Down/Drag/Up`.
- Render: highlight the selected range with inverse-video / reverse-
  color cell style.
- OSC52 copy: on mouse-release after drag (or `Ctrl-Shift-C`), base64-
  encode the selection and write `\x1b]52;c;<base64>\x07` to stdout.
- Click-to-position-cursor: on `MouseEvent::Down` without drag, move
  the textarea cursor to the clicked character.
- In-form paging: bind `Ctrl-U`/`Ctrl-D` (and arrow keys) to move the
  textarea's internal viewport for long bodies — PageUp/Down are now
  globally claimed by Zellij scrollback (Phase 2).
- Mouse wheel: route to the form's own scroll position.

Bracketed paste, Ctrl-chord forwarding, and soft wrap from `5eb228c`
stay — the new work composes on top.

Verification: end-to-end smoke test in scratchpad #91 (steps 1-8).

If this todo gets large in flight, consider splitting selection/render/
OSC52 from click-to-position from in-form paging into separate todos —
they're independent sub-pieces.

content.text: ok

- **#94** Phase 2: global PageUp/PageDown → ScrollUp/ScrollDown (~2 lines per mode, `panopt/src/up.rs`)
- **#95** Phase 3: in-form mouse / OSC52 / Ctrl-U/Ctrl-D in `panopt _viewer` (the bulk — `viewer.rs`)

No blockers wired between them; they're independent. Phase 3 is the candidate for further splitting if you want sub-todos for selection/OSC52/click-position/paging — let me know.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the comprehensive report:

---

## Phase 1 Planning Report: Auto-Locked Mode + Mouse Mode for PANopt Cockpit

### 1. **PaneUpdate Handling Structure** (`crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs`)

**Current state tracking:**
- **Lines 418-421**: Two fields track focus state across `PaneUpdate` calls:
  - `sidebar_focused: bool` — whether any plugin pane is currently focused
  - `focused_tab: Option<usize>` — the tab position containing the focused pane

- **Lines 1000-1054** (`ingest_panes` method): The manifest iteration that builds these:
  ```
  Lines 1011-1019: Check p.is_focused to detect focus
    - If focused and p.is_plugin → sidebar_focused_this_update = true
    - If focused and not plugin → focused_non_plugin = Some(id)
  Lines 1021-1043: Skip plugin panes, iterate content panes only
    - Extract p.id, p.is_focused, p.is_plugin, p.is_selectable
    - Build PaneRow entries with p.is_focused, p.is_suppressed, p.exited
  Lines 1052-1054: Update state only if saw_focused_pane=true
    - self.sidebar_focused = sidebar_focused_this_update
    - self.focused_tab = focused_tab_this_update
  ```

**Key insight:** The plugin **already tracks prior focus state** (lines 418, 1053-1054). You can detect a transition by comparing `self.sidebar_focused` (old) vs `sidebar_focused_this_update` (new) **within the same `ingest_panes` call**, before updating `self.sidebar_focused`. This enables emitting mode switches only on transitions, not every tick.

**Pane differentiation:**
- `p.is_plugin` field identifies sidebar plugin panes
- Content panes have `is_plugin=false` and `is_selectable=true`
- The `focused_non_plugin` variable (line 1026) captures the currently focused content pane id

---

### 2. **Mode-Switch API & Permissions**

**Existing permission setup** (lines 554-558):
```rust
request_permission(&[
    PermissionType::ReadApplicationState,
    PermissionType::ChangeApplicationState,  // ← Already granted
    PermissionType::RunCommands,
]);
```
✅ **`ChangeApplicationState` permission is already requested** — no permission changes needed.

**Mode-switch function:** The zellij-tile 0.44 API exports `switch_to_mode(InputMode)` from `zellij_tile::prelude::*` (line 36). This function is not yet used in the plugin but is available. You'll call it after transitioning:
- Content pane focused → `switch_to_mode(InputMode::Locked)`
- Sidebar pane focused → `switch_to_mode(InputMode::Normal)`

**No other mode-switching or `intercept_key_presses` calls exist** in the codebase yet.

---

### 3. **Mouse Mode**

**Status:** Not currently set anywhere in the codebase.

- **`crates/panopt/src/up.rs:render_config`** (lines 418-473): Rewrites the base Zellij config but does not touch `mouse_mode`. 
- **`crates/panopt/src/up.rs` constants** (lines 96-148): Handle scroll, new-pane, and close bindings via string replacements. No `mouse_mode` constant yet.
- **Zellij default:** The stock Zellij config enables `mouse_mode true` by default; the generated cockpit config will inherit it.

**For Phase 1:** Confirm `mouse_mode true` is present in the generated config. If needed, a single-line addition to `render_config` after line 468 will ensure it:
```rust
// After the body variable is constructed, before writing:
let body = format!("...{tweaked}\nmouse_mode true\n");
```

---

### 4. **Existing Zellij API Usage**

**zellij-tile version:** 0.44 (line 11 of `Cargo.toml`)

**API calls already in use:**
- `request_permission(...)` — line 554
- `subscribe(...)` — line 559
- `close_pane_with_id(pane)` — lines 1561, 1637, 1668, 1672
- `focus_pane_with_id(pane, ...)` — lines 598, 1461, 1718, 1733
- `open_command_pane(...)`, `open_command_pane_floating(...)` — for spawning panes
- `run_command_with_env_variables_and_cwd(...)` — line 1685

**No existing mode-switching.** `switch_to_mode` and `InputMode` are not imported or used; they're part of zellij-tile's public API but untouched in panopt-zellij.

---

### 5. **Critical File & Line References for Implementation**

| **File** | **Lines** | **Purpose** |
|----------|-----------|-----------|
| `crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs:418-421` | State tracking | `sidebar_focused`, `focused_tab` declarations |
| `crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs:554-558` | Permissions | `request_permission` call (already has `ChangeApplicationState`) |
| `crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs:995-1054` | PaneUpdate handler | `ingest_panes` method: manifest iteration, focus detection, state update |
| `crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs:1011-1019` | Focus logic | Where to inject the mode-switch call: detect `p.is_focused && !p.is_plugin` → `Locked`, or fall through to `Normal` |
| `crates/panopt/src/up.rs:96-148` | Binding constants | Existing `SCROLL_BIND_FROM`, `NEW_PANE_BIND_FROM`, etc.; PageUp/PageDown bindings will slot here |
| `crates/panopt/src/up.rs:418-473` | Config generation | `render_config` function; add `mouse_mode true` after line 468 |

**Implementation plan snapshot:**
1. In `ingest_panes`, after building `sidebar_focused_this_update` but before updating `self.sidebar_focused`, check if the focus changed.
2. On transition `sidebar_focused(old) → focused_content(new)`: call `switch_to_mode(InputMode::Locked)`.
3. On transition `focused_content(old) → sidebar_focused(new)`: call `switch_to_mode(InputMode::Normal)`.
4. In `render_config`, add `mouse_mode true` to the generated KDL config string.
5. (Phase 2) Add PageUp/PageDown→ScrollUp/ScrollDown bindings to `up.rs` constants + `retarget_*` logic.

| `crates/panopt/src/up.rs:96-148` | Binding constants | Existing `SCROLL_BIND_FROM`, `NEW_PANE_BIND_FROM`, etc.; PageUp/PageDown bindings will slot here |
| `crates/panopt/src/up.rs:418-473` | Config generation | `render_config` function; add `mouse_mode true` after line 468 |

**Implementation plan snapshot:**
1. In `ingest_panes`, after building `sidebar_focused_this_update` but before updating `self.sidebar_focused`, check if the focus changed.
2. On transition `sidebar_focused(old) → focused_content(new)`: call `switch_to_mode(InputMode::Locked)`.
3. On transition `focused_content(old) → sidebar_focused(new)`: call `switch_to_mode(InputMode::Normal)`.
4. In `render_config`, add `mouse_mode true` to the generated KDL config string.
5. (Phase 2) Add PageUp/PageDown→ScrollUp/ScrollDown bindings to `up.rs` constants + `retarget_*` logic.

1	//! PANopt coordination sidebar - a Zellij plugin.
2	//!
3	//! Each plugin pane renders one kind of resource - todos, agents, terminals,
4	//! commands, or scratchpads - selected by the `mode` value in its layout
5	//! config. The five panes stack vertically in the cockpit's left column, each
6	//! pinned to its own fixed proportion so adding or removing panes on the
7	//! right cannot reshape any of them. A keyboard cursor walks the pane's items
8	//! and scrolls when it hits the visible window's edge; the mouse clicks any
9	//! row.
10	//!
11	//! The cockpit is these five panes plus one content pane on the right.
12	//! Selecting an item swaps its pane into that one slot and suppresses
13	//! whatever was there - a suppressed pane keeps running, just hidden, no
14	//! stack and no title bar. Documents (todos, scratchpads, lists) all share
15	//! one re-pointable `panopt _viewer` pane; agents, commands, and terminals
16	//! are each their own pane. Moving the cursor previews the selected item in
17	//! the slot - or clears the slot when the row has nothing to show - always
18	//! without taking focus off the plugin pane. A click does the same; Enter
19	//! additionally focuses the pane.
20	//!
21	//! If the user splits the content pane, a selection swaps into whichever
22	//! pane was focused last before any plugin pane took focus - the designated
23	//! slot. Each of the five panes derives the slot independently from the same
24	//! `PaneUpdate` manifest, so they all converge on the same target.
25	//!
26	//! The Todos plugin pane doubles as the cockpit gatekeeper: it is the only
27	//! pane that handles the close-request and spawn pipes (see `up::render_config`).
28	//! When an active agent, command, or terminal would be lost, the Todos pane
29	//! refuses by showing a floating dialog with a `close anyway` override; any
30	//! of the five plugin panes themselves cannot be closed.
31	
32	use std::collections::BTreeMap;
33	use std::fs;
34	use std::path::PathBuf;
35	
36	use zellij_tile::prelude::*;
37	
38	/// Routing slot prefix for viewer panes the plugin spawns ad hoc. The layout
39	/// boots one viewer with `--slot main`; further viewers spawned by
40	/// [`PanoptPane::ensure_viewer_in_slot`] get unique names `v<mode-letter><n>`
41	/// (`vt1`, `vs1`, `va1`, ...) so each pane has its own
42	/// `.panopt/.cockpit/viewer-<slot>.json` routing file and the five plugin
43	/// instances cannot collide on the same suffix. Per-pane routing keeps
44	/// sidebar navigation single-pane: only the slot's viewer re-points on a
45	/// preview, leaving any other split's viewer on whatever it was last showing
46	/// (the user's "kept doc" pattern).
47	const SPAWNED_VIEWER_SLOT_PREFIX: &str = "v";
48	
49	/// Per-pane file the cockpit projects agent labels into. Each plugin instance
50	/// writes the labels it owns (the Todos pane gets named agents via the
51	/// `panopt:spawn-agent` pipe) and every instance reads the file so labels
52	/// stay consistent across the five panes.
53	const AGENT_LABELS_PATH: &str = "/host/.panopt/.cockpit/agent-labels.json";
54	
55	/// Which kind of resource one plugin pane renders. Five plugin instances run
56	/// in parallel, one per `Mode`, each configured by the `mode "<kind>"` value
57	/// in the layout's plugin block. Zellij keys plugin identity on
58	/// `(URL, configuration)`, so the five panes are five distinct instances and
59	/// can be addressed individually by `zellij action pipe --plugin-configuration
60	/// "mode=<kind>"`.

410	
411	    /// Whether any plugin pane is currently the focused pane in its tab.
412	    /// Updated by [`PanoptPane::ingest_panes`] but only from a non-transient
413	    /// manifest: a transient `zellij action pipe` pane briefly steals focus
414	    /// while the close-gate pipes fly, and we must not let that flicker make
415	    /// the gate think the user has moved off the cockpit panes.
416	    /// Used by [`PanoptPane::gate_close_focus`] to refuse closing any plugin
417	    /// pane absolutely.
418	    sidebar_focused: bool,
419	    /// The tab position with a focused pane, derived from the same manifest
420	    /// snapshot that drives `sidebar_focused`. Scopes the CloseTab gate.
421	    focused_tab: Option<usize>,
422	    /// The last gate refusal: what was refused (a label), set when the gate
423	    /// blocks an action because active items would be lost. Surfaced through
424	    /// the pane's frame title so the user knows their keypress was
425	    /// intercepted. Cleared on the next successful navigation. Only the Todos
426	    /// pane runs the gate, so only the Todos pane ever sets this.
427	    last_gate_refusal: Option<String>,
428	    /// The frame title most recently pushed to Zellij via
429	    /// [`rename_plugin_pane`]. Kept so we only re-issue the host call when
430	    /// the title actually changes (gate refusal appears/clears, scroll
431	    /// position shifts, ...) rather than on every render tick.
432	    last_frame_title: String,
433	    /// Last terminal-pane title we pushed via [`rename_terminal_pane`],
434	    /// keyed by Zellij terminal pane id. Lets the Todos pane (the only one
435	    /// that titles right-pane terminals) re-issue the host call only when
436	    /// the title actually changes, rather than on every manifest tick.
437	    last_pane_titles: BTreeMap<u32, String>,
438	    /// Whether the initial preview has been shown on startup. Only the Todos
439	    /// pane drives the initial preview; the others remain idle until the
440	    /// user navigates them.
441	    initial_preview_done: bool,
442	    /// Counter for delaying the initial preview until the UI is ready.
443	    initial_preview_delay: u32,
444	    /// When `true`, the pane body shows the per-mode key cheat-sheet instead
445	    /// of the item list. Toggled by `?`; any other keypress dismisses it.
446	    /// Holds the same set of keys across modes so the UI never carries
447	    /// per-mode hint clutter - the help is the only place keys are listed.
448	    show_help: bool,
449	}
450	
451	/// A parsed `.panopt/processes.md` line. The line format is preserved from
452	/// the pre-V6 `roster.md` so the existing `[kind] #id label` parser still
453	/// works; any trailing `(from #N)` is dropped from `label`.
454	struct ProcessRow {
455	    kind: String,
456	    id: u64,
457	    label: String,
458	}

content.text:
All 15 up.rs tests pass, including the 3 new ones for `ensure_mouse_mode_enabled`.

**Phase 1 (#93) — code changes complete on `right-pane-terminal-ux-phase1`:**

1. **`crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs`** — Added `last_emitted_locked: Option<bool>` field on `PanoptPane` (line ~449). In `ingest_panes`, after the existing `if saw_focused_pane` block, gated to `Mode::Todos`: compute `want_locked = !sidebar_focused_this_update`, fire `switch_to_input_mode(&InputMode::Locked)` or `&InputMode::Normal` only when the decision changes. The Todos pane is the sole emitter so the other four plugins don't issue redundant calls.

2. **`crates/panopt/src/up.rs`** — Added `MOUSE_MODE_OFF`/`MOUSE_MODE_ON` constants + `ensure_mouse_mode_enabled` helper. Called from `render_config` after the close-bindings rewrite. Helper flips an explicit `mouse_mode false`, appends `mouse_mode true` only when neither form is present, and is idempotent if already on. Three unit tests cover the cases. The generated-config comment now mentions the forced mouse mode and links it to the auto-lock behavior.

**Verification status:**
- `just check` PASS · `just clippy` PASS · `cargo test -p panopt up::` — 15/15 PASS.
- Runtime smoke test from scratchpad #91 (focus a viewer pane → Locked, focus sidebar → Normal; bare-drag in a viewer pane should reach the form) needs a fresh cockpit: `just refresh && panopt up`. I did not run that — Phase 1 alone enables the mechanism, but the form-side selection model from Phase 3 is what makes the drag visibly do something.

Not committed yet, and todo #93 is still `in_progress`. Tell me to commit and `mac` it once you've poked at the cockpit (or now if you'd rather defer the live test).

Phase 1 of #93: auto-lock Zellij input mode on content focus

The cockpit's right-side panes (the `panopt _viewer` form and any
agent / terminal) should feel like ordinary terminal sessions for the
gestures users actually reach for. Step one is to get Zellij's keybinds
out of the way the moment focus lands on one of those panes, so keys
flow straight to the inner program; on focus return to a sidebar
plugin pane the binds come back so the cockpit is navigable again.

- `panopt-zellij`: new `last_emitted_locked` field on `PanoptPane`;
  `ingest_panes` fires `switch_to_input_mode(&InputMode::Locked)` or
  `&InputMode::Normal` on a sidebar/content transition, gated to the
  Todos pane so the other four plugin instances don't issue redundant
  calls. The decision-cache makes a no-transition `PaneUpdate` (resize,
  title change, ...) a true no-op rather than yanking the user out of
  an explicit Zellij mode.
- `panopt/up.rs`: `ensure_mouse_mode_enabled` forces `mouse_mode true`
  into the generated cockpit config - flipping an explicit
  `mouse_mode false` if present, appending the line iff neither form is
  already there, and idempotent when already on. Without this the
  locked-mode flip could strand a user whose own config disabled mouse
  mode, since their content panes would no longer receive the events
  Phase 3's in-form selection / OSC52 work depends on.

See scratchpad #91 for the three-phase plan; this is piece 1.
EOF
)" && git status

input.command:
git diff --stat && echo "---" && git add crates/panopt/src/up.rs && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Keep Alt-arrow nav and rewritten Alt-N / Ctrl-Q alive in Locked mode

The previous commit auto-locks the multiplexer when focus lands on a
content pane, which made keys flow to the inner program as intended -
but it also stripped every `shared_except "locked"` binding, including
the Alt-arrow pane navigation users reach for to leave the pane they
just entered. Ctrl-G to escape Locked is no escape either: the plugin
just re-locks them on the next PaneUpdate.

Append a `keybinds { locked { ... } }` block to the generated cockpit
config that re-binds:

- Alt arrows -> MoveFocusOrTab / MoveFocus, matching the stock Zellij
  default (with tab fall-through at row edges for horizontal).
- Alt-N -> the same `panopt:spawn-blank-pane` pipe the outside-locked
  rewrite uses, so spawning still goes through the gatekeeper.
- Ctrl-Q -> the same `panopt:quit-request` pipe, so quitting still hits
  the close gate.

Zellij merges multiple keybinds blocks additively, so appending a
second block - rather than string-replacing the base config's existing
`locked { ... }` - sidesteps the casing brittleness between the stock
dump (`SwitchToMode "Normal"`) and hand-edited configs (`"normal"`).

Alt-h / Alt-j / Alt-k / Alt-l are deliberately left bound to Zellij in
shared_except "locked" but NOT carried into locked; Alt-h is bash's
`backward-kill-word` and shouldn't be eaten by Zellij when a shell pane
is focused.
EOF
)" && git log --oneline -3

content.content:
# Default recipe: list everything.
default:
    @just --list

# Build the workspace (debug).
build:
    cargo build --workspace

# Build the workspace (release).
release:
    cargo build --workspace --release

# Build the Zellij sidebar plugin (wasm32-wasip1).
plugin:
    cargo build --manifest-path crates/panopt-zellij/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-wasip1

# Build the Zellij sidebar plugin in release mode.
plugin-release:
    cargo build --manifest-path crates/panopt-zellij/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-wasip1 --release

# Run workspace tests.
test:
    cargo test --workspace

# Format all crates (workspace + plugin).
fmt:
    cargo fmt --all
    cargo fmt --manifest-path crates/panopt-zellij/Cargo.toml

# Check formatting without modifying files.
fmt-check:
    cargo fmt --all -- --check
    cargo fmt --manifest-path crates/panopt-zellij/Cargo.toml -- --check

# Clippy across the workspace and the plugin, warnings as errors.
clippy:
    cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings
    cargo clippy --manifest-path crates/panopt-zellij/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-wasip1 -- -D warnings

# Fast type-check (workspace + plugin).
check:
    cargo check --workspace
    cargo check --manifest-path crates/panopt-zellij/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-wasip1

# Install panopt + panoptd to ~/.cargo/bin.
install:
    cargo install --path crates/panopt
    cargo install --path crates/panoptd

# Run the daemon in the foreground (logs to stderr).
daemon:
    cargo run -p panoptd -- --port 7600

# Tail the daemon log file written by `panopt up`.
logs:
    tail -F ~/.local/share/panopt/panoptd.log

# If a daemon is already running on port 7600, `panopt up` connects to it
# rather than restarting it - use `just stop && just up` to force a fresh
# debug daemon, or `just restart-daemon` to swap the daemon out without
# touching the cockpit.
#
# Launch the cockpit in the current project (debug binaries). Build the
# workspace AND the release wasm plugin first so `panoptd` and the sidebar
# plugin both reflect the latest sources; without these, `cargo run -p
# panopt` would happily re-spawn yesterday's `panoptd` from `target/debug/`
# and Zellij would load yesterday's plugin from
# `crates/panopt-zellij/target/wasm32-wasip1/release/panopt-zellij.wasm`
# (the path `panopt up` auto-detects in `default_plugin_wasm`).
up: plugin-release
    cargo build --workspace
    cargo run -p panopt -- up

# Stop the running panoptd (SIGTERM x2 to clear the clients-connected gate).
stop:
    -pkill -TERM -x panoptd
    @sleep 1
    -pkill -TERM -x panoptd

# Nuke EVERYTHING and start over: kill every panopt-* Zellij session (which

# Build the Zellij sidebar plugin in release mode.
plugin-release:
    cargo build --manifest-path crates/panopt-zellij/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-wasip1 --release

# Run workspace tests.
test:
    cargo test --workspace

# Format all crates (workspace + plugin).
fmt:
    cargo fmt --all
    cargo fmt --manifest-path crates/panopt-zellij/Cargo.toml

# Check formatting without modifying files.
fmt-check:
    cargo fmt --all -- --check
    cargo fmt --manifest-path crates/panopt-zellij/Cargo.toml -- --check

# Clippy across the workspace and the plugin, warnings as errors.
clippy:
    cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings
    cargo clippy --manifest-path crates/panopt-zellij/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-wasip1 -- -D warnings

# Fast type-check (workspace + plugin).
check:
    cargo check --workspace
    cargo check --manifest-path crates/panopt-zellij/Cargo.toml --target wasm32-wasip1

# Install panopt + panoptd to ~/.cargo/bin.
install:
    cargo install --path crates/panopt
    cargo install --path crates/panoptd

# Run the daemon in the foreground (logs to stderr).
daemon:
    cargo run -p panoptd -- --port 7600

# Tail the daemon log file written by `panopt up`.
logs:
    tail -F ~/.local/share/panopt/panoptd.log

# If a daemon is already running on port 7600, `panopt up` connects to it
# rather than restarting it - use `just stop && just up` to force a fresh
# debug daemon, or `just restart-daemon` to swap the daemon out without
# touching the cockpit.
#
# Launch the cockpit in the current project (debug binaries). Build the
# workspace AND the release wasm plugin first so `panoptd` and the sidebar
# plugin both reflect the latest sources; without these, `cargo run -p
# panopt` would happily re-spawn yesterday's `panoptd` from `target/debug/`
# and Zellij would load yesterday's plugin from
# `crates/panopt-zellij/target/wasm32-wasip1/release/panopt-zellij.wasm`
# (the path `panopt up` auto-detects in `default_plugin_wasm`).
up: plugin-release
    cargo build --workspace
    cargo run -p panopt -- up

# Stop the running panoptd (SIGTERM x2 to clear the clients-connected gate).
stop:
    -pkill -TERM -x panoptd
    @sleep 1
    -pkill -TERM -x panoptd

input.command:
git add crates/panopt/src/up.rs && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Splice locked-mode binds into the existing keybinds block, not append

The previous commit appended a second `keybinds { locked { ... } }`
block to the generated cockpit config, expecting Zellij to merge it
with the base config's existing locked block. It does not - the KDL
parser at `zellij-utils/src/kdl/mod.rs:4862` carries a TODO that
literally says "handle cases where we have more than one of these
blocks (eg. two `keybinds`)" and silently ignores the second node. So
the auto-lock landed without Alt-arrow navigation, exactly as before.

This is especially load-bearing for any config that carries
`clear-defaults=true` (the more useful case, where the user has
hand-rolled their full keybinds) - those configs have no Zellij
default shared blocks for Locked mode to inherit from.

Replace with `inject_locked_keybinds`: walk the base config, find the
first `locked {` header, balance braces to its matching closing `}`,
and splice the cockpit-essential binds in just before it. Reuses the
same Alt-N / Ctrl-Q pipe-gated rewrites so the gatekeeper still sees
spawn and quit. Warns if no locked block exists so a user with an
unconventional config knows their content panes will be inert under
auto-lock until they wire the binds themselves.

Three unit tests cover the happy path, the brace-balancing through the
nested action block, and the no-locked-block warning case.
EOF
)" && git log --oneline -5

content.text:
Committed `b4be4d5`. The intermediate `37db386` was correct in intent but Zellij silently ignored the appended block — the new commit splices into the existing `locked` block, balancing braces.

**To pick this up:** drop out of the cockpit entirely (Ctrl-Q to leave Zellij, or `zellij delete-session` from a plain shell), then `just refresh` from outside Zellij. `panopt up` on an attached session reuses the previously generated `cockpit-config.kdl`.

If Alt-Left still doesn't fire after a clean restart, the generated config lives at the path `paths::cockpit_config()` resolves to (likely `~/.local/share/panopt/cockpit-config.kdl`) — grep it for `bind "Alt Left"` to confirm the splice landed.

input.command:
git add crates/panopt/src/up.rs && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Phase 2 of #94: PageUp / PageDown scroll directly in every mode

The cockpit wants PageUp / PageDown to scroll a pane's Zellij scrollback
without first switching into scroll mode, and to do that uniformly
across every input mode so the gesture is transparent to the user (and
to the inner program - we don't want Claude to see PageUp and repurpose
it for prompt history).

`inject_page_scroll_keybinds` walks the base config, finds the first
top-level `keybinds` node, skips past its attributes (e.g.
`clear-defaults=true`) to its opening brace, balances braces to the
matching close, and splices a `shared { bind "PageUp" { ScrollUp; }
bind "PageDown" { ScrollDown; } }` block in just before it. The
`shared` block applies to every mode including locked, which is exactly
what we want for content panes the plugin has auto-locked. Direct
`ScrollUp` / `ScrollDown` actions (rather than `SwitchToMode "scroll"`)
keep the gesture modal-free: Zellij scrolls the pane and auto-returns
to the bottom on the next input or output.

Mirrors `inject_locked_keybinds`'s strategy because the same Zellij
constraint applies here: a second top-level `keybinds` block is
silently ignored. Three new tests cover the happy-path splice, the
`clear-defaults=true` attribute case (where the brace finder must skip
attribute tokens), and the no-`keybinds`-block warning.
EOF
)" && git log --oneline -5

internal viewport for long bodies.
EOF

{"started":true,"todo":{"assignee":"","blockers":[],"body":"Phase 3 of scratchpad #91 ("Right-pane regular terminal UX"). The bulk
of the work: make `panopt _viewer` own its own selection/copy/paging
UX so it doesn't depend on Zellij's modal scroll/copy modes.

Sub-pieces (all in `crates/panopt/src/viewer.rs` and the form/textarea
modules it depends on):

- Enable mouse capture in the crossterm setup (`EnableMouseCapture`).
- Selection model: track `Option<(start, end)>` character positions in
  each focused textarea / browsable region; update on
  `MouseEvent::Down/Drag/Up`.
- Render: highlight the selected range with inverse-video / reverse-
  color cell style.
- OSC52 copy: on mouse-release after drag (or `Ctrl-Shift-C`), base64-
  encode the selection and write `\x1b]52;c;<base64>\x07` to stdout.
- Click-to-position-cursor: on `MouseEvent::Down` without drag, move
  the textarea cursor to the clicked character.
- In-form paging: bind `Ctrl-U`/`Ctrl-D` (and arrow keys) to move the
  textarea's internal viewport for long bodies — PageUp/Down are now
  globally claimed by Zellij scrollback (Phase 2).
- Mouse wheel: route to the form's own scroll position.

Bracketed paste, Ctrl-chord forwarding, and soft wrap from `5eb228c`
stay — the new work composes on top.

Verification: end-to-end smoke test in scratchpad #91 (steps 1-8).

If this todo gets large in flight, consider splitting selection/render/
OSC52 from click-to-position from in-form paging into separate todos —

input.description: Map viewer.rs and form text input architecture
input.prompt:
I need to plan Phase 3 of scratchpad #91 (#95 in panopt). The task: give `panopt _viewer` regular-terminal UX — mouse selection + OSC52 copy + Ctrl-U/Ctrl-D internal paging in form panes. Background: PageUp/PageDown were just bound globally to Zellij PageScrollUp/PageScrollDown (a `shared { ... }` block in the cockpit config); the form panes are on the terminal's alt-screen so Zellij's scrollback is empty there. Form panes need their OWN paging gesture (Ctrl-U/Ctrl-D), their own mouse selection, and OSC52 copy.

Please read the code and report (concise, under 600 words):

1. **`crates/panopt/src/viewer.rs` shape**: It's 1736 lines. What's the top-level structure? Where is the crossterm setup (`enable_raw_mode`, `EnterAlternateScreen`, `EnableBracketedPaste`, etc.) — is `EnableMouseCapture` already on? What handles `KeyEvent` and what handles `MouseEvent`? Show the event-loop's match arms by line number.

2. **Form layer**: `todo_form.rs` (2075 lines) and `scratchpad_form.rs` (795 lines). What text-input crate are they using? Are they tui-textarea, or is there a hand-rolled textarea? Where do they own their cursor position and viewport offset? Is there already any concept of vertical scrolling within a long body field? Look at the imports too.

3. **Existing mouse handling**: Grep for `MouseEvent`, `MouseEventKind`, `Mouse(`, `EnableMouseCapture`, `disable_mouse_capture`. List every site, indicate whether the form/viewer currently routes mouse events anywhere.

4. **Bracketed paste / Ctrl-chord forwarding from `5eb228c`**: How does the viewer currently handle paste and Ctrl chords? Show the dispatch path - I need to understand what the new Ctrl-U/Ctrl-D handlers should sit alongside.

5. **Render layer**: What renders the form bodies? Is it ratatui's `Paragraph`, a tui-textarea widget, custom Lines? Where would a "selected range highlight" go — show the relevant render function and line numbers. Where would a "scroll offset" for long bodies be applied?

6. **Wrap module**: There's a `wrap.rs` (244 lines) — what does it do? Soft-wrap helper? Important for "where am I in the wrapped text" coordinates needed for click-to-position and selection.

7. **OSC52 / clipboard**: any existing clipboard or OSC52 path in the codebase? Grep for "OSC", "52", "clipboard", "copypasta", `\x1b]52`.

8. **Recommendation**: based on what you see, which sub-piece looks like the smallest self-contained first commit? My instinct is Ctrl-U/Ctrl-D in-form paging (smallest, most independent), then mouse capture + click-to-position, then selection model + OSC52. Tell me if that ordering matches the code's seams, or if there's a forced sequence (e.g. mouse capture has to land first because the dispatch routing changes).

Read-only. Give file:line refs liberally.

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have enough information. Let me compile the comprehensive report:

---

## Phase 3 Planning: Form Panes UX (Mouse + OSC52 + Ctrl-U/D Paging)

### 1. **`viewer.rs` Structure (1736 lines)**

**Top-level structure:**
- Lines 1-15: Module doc
- Lines 22-38: Imports (crossterm event handling, ratatui, MCP client)
- Lines 40-45: Constants (TICK, REFRESH, DEBOUNCE durations)
- Lines 47-122: `Target` enum + helpers (parse, key, is_list, is_form)
- Lines 124-143: `Content` enum (Doc, List, Message, TodoForm, ScratchpadForm)
- Lines 145-171: `ListEntry` struct
- Lines 173-255: `TodoFilter` enum + filter logic
- Lines 256-320: `TodoSort` enum + sort logic
- Lines 434-470: `Viewer` struct
- Lines 472-1700+: impl Viewer

**Crossterm setup:**
- **Line 419**: `execute!(stdout(), EnableBracketedPaste, EnableMouseCapture)` — **both enabled at startup**
  - Bracketed paste is active and works (5eb228c added the supporting code path)
  - Mouse capture is active but only list mode routes mouse events (forms ignore them, lines 635–657)

**Event loop match arms (lines 528–536):**
```
Event::Key(key) if key.kind == KeyEventKind::Press && self.handle_key(key) => return Ok(())
Event::Paste(s) => self.handle_paste(&s)
Event::Mouse(m) => self.handle_mouse(m)
Event::Resize(_, _) => self.needs_draw = true
```

**Key handler:** `handle_key` (lines 547–613)
- Forms delegate to their own `handle_key` (TodoForm, ScratchpadForm)
- List mode: Ctrl-C, q, arrows/hjkl, PageUp/Down, Home/End, f/F (filter), s/S/d/D (sort), Enter (open)

**Mouse handler:** `handle_mouse` (lines 635–658)
- Only list mode: Left-click selects and opens a row
- ScrollUp/ScrollDown move cursor in list/doc modes
- Forms explicitly match nothing — they ignore mouse events entirely (line 656)

---

### 2. **Form Layer: Text Input & Cursor/Viewport Management**

**Both forms use `tui_textarea::TextArea` 0.7:**
- `todo_form.rs` imports: line 27: `use tui_textarea::TextArea;`
- `scratchpad_form.rs` imports: line 31: `use tui_textarea::TextArea;`

**Cursor and viewport ownership:**
- **todo_form.rs:**
  - Line 183-184: `body_scroll: usize` — manual viewport offset for body field
  - The textarea owns `cursor()` (line 1175: `self.body.cursor()`)
  - Wrapped display maps logical cursor to visual position (wrap.rs handles this)
  
- **scratchpad_form.rs:**
  - Line 110-111: `body_scroll: usize` — same manual offset
  - Same pattern: textarea owns cursor, manual wrap-aware viewport (lines 514–516)

**Vertical scrolling within body field:**
- **Already implemented:** Both forms compute `body_scroll` dynamically in `draw_body`
  - **todo_form.rs lines 1179–1189:** Cursor auto-scrolls viewport; clamped to max_scroll
  - **scratchpad_form.rs lines 518–525:** Identical logic
  - The scroll is **cursor-follow**, not independent pagination — there is no existing Ctrl-U/Ctrl-D mechanism

---

### 3. **Existing Mouse Handling**

**Grep results for MouseEvent / MouseEventKind / EnableMouseCapture / disable_mouse_capture:**

```
viewer.rs:23   [imports]
viewer.rs:24   [imports]
viewer.rs:419  execute!(stdout(), EnableBracketedPaste, EnableMouseCapture)
viewer.rs:533  Event::Mouse(m) => self.handle_mouse(m)
viewer.rs:635  fn handle_mouse(&mut self, m: MouseEvent) { ... }
viewer.rs:637    MouseEventKind::Down(MouseButton::Left) => { list row routing }
viewer.rs:644    MouseEventKind::ScrollUp => { list/doc scroll }
viewer.rs:650    MouseEventKind::ScrollDown => { list/doc scroll }
```

**Current routing:**
- `MouseCapture` is **enabled globally** at startup (line 419)
- `handle_mouse` is called for every `Event::Mouse` in the event loop (line 533)
- **List mode only:** Left-click and scroll wheel (lines 637–655)
- **Forms:** No match arms — mouse events are silently dropped inside forms

**Forms do NOT route mouse at all:** The viewer's `handle_mouse` only branches on list/doc, not forms (line 645, 651). When a form is focused, mouse events arrive but are ignored.

---

### 4. **Bracketed Paste & Ctrl Chord Forwarding (from 5eb228c)**

**Bracketed paste dispatch (lines 615–628):**
```rust
fn handle_paste(&mut self, s: &str) {
    match &mut self.content {
        Content::TodoForm(form) => {
            let _ = form.handle_paste(s);
            self.needs_draw = true;
        }
        Content::ScratchpadForm(form) => {
            let _ = form.handle_paste(s);
            self.needs_draw = true;
        }
        _ => {}
    }
}
```

**Forms' paste handling:**
- **todo_form.rs lines 353–388:** `handle_paste` routes to focused field
- **scratchpad_form.rs lines 221–236:** Same pattern
- Single-line fields use `paste_into_single_line`, multi-line use `paste_into`
- Both defined in todo_form.rs (lines 1529–1556) and re-exported

**Ctrl chord forwarding in text input (todo_form.rs lines 1501–1525):**
```rust
pub(crate) fn text_input(area: &mut TextArea, key: KeyEvent) -> bool {
    let ctrl = key.modifiers.contains(KeyModifiers::CONTROL);
    match key.code {
        KeyCode::Char('j') | KeyCode::Char('m') if ctrl => {
            area.insert_newline();  // Ctrl-J/M for newline
            true
        }
        KeyCode::Char('z') if ctrl => area.undo(),  // Ctrl-Z for undo
        _ => area.input(key),  // Everything else to textarea
    }
}
```

**Dispatch path for new Ctrl chords:**
- In `text_input` (line 1501), add branches before the fallback `area.input(key)` at line 1509
- For body field: `ScalarField::Body` branch in `scalar_input_key` (line 412) calls `text_input`
- For scratchpad body: `Field::Body` in `scratchpad_form.rs:field_key` (line 209) calls `text_input`

---

### 5. **Render Layer: Body Field Display & Scroll Offset Application**

**todo_form.rs `draw_body` (lines 1156–1205):**
```rust
fn draw_body(&mut self, frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect) {
    let cursor = self.body.cursor();
    let wrapped = crate::wrap::wrap_for_display(self.body.lines(), cursor, width);
    let (cvr, cvc) = wrapped.cursor;
    
    // Auto-adjust body_scroll to keep cursor in view
    if cvr < self.body_scroll { self.body_scroll = cvr; }
    else if cvr >= self.body_scroll + height { self.body_scroll = cvr + 1 - height; }
    let max_scroll = wrapped.lines.len().saturating_sub(height);
    if self.body_scroll > max_scroll { self.body_scroll = max_scroll; }
    
    // Render visible slice
    let visible: Vec<Line> = wrapped.lines
        .iter()
        .skip(self.body_scroll)
        .take(height)
        .map(|l| Line::from(l.clone()))
        .collect();
    frame.render_widget(Paragraph::new(visible), inner);
    
    // Overlay cursor
    if focused && cvr >= self.body_scroll && cvr < self.body_scroll + height && cvc < width {
        let cy = inner.y + (cvr - self.body_scroll) as u16;
        let cx = inner.x + cvc as u16;
        frame.set_cursor_position((cx, cy));
    }
}
```

**scratchpad_form.rs `draw_body` (lines 495–542):** Identical pattern.

**Where to apply selection highlight:**
- The visible lines (lines 1191–1197 todo_form, lines 528–534 scratchpad_form) are `Vec<Line>`
- Selection would need to map logical start/end positions through `wrap_for_display` to visual ranges
- Highlight applied in the `Span` level within each visible `Line` before render
- **Critical:** wrap.rs (lines 1–244) already maps cursor; selection would need a companion function like `wrap_for_selection(lines, cursor, start, end, width)` that returns a Vec of (row_idx, col_range) tuples

---

### 6. **wrap.rs Module (244 lines)**

**Purpose:** Soft-wrap helper for body fields.

**Key structure:**
```rust
pub(crate) struct Wrapped {
    pub lines: Vec<String>,        // Visual rows
    pub cursor: (usize, usize),    // (visual_row, visual_col)
}

pub(crate) fn wrap_for_display(
    lines: &[String],
    cursor: (usize, usize),
    width: usize
) -> Wrapped
```

**What it does:**
- **Lines 37–128:** Word-wrap with fallback to char-wrap for long tokens
- **Lines 22–30:** Logical-to-visual cursor mapping (ensures 1-to-1 char correspondence)
- **Lines 131–136:** Helper `visual_col_within` for cell-width calculation
- **Lines 18:** Uses `unicode_width::UnicodeWidthChar` for precise cell widths

**For Phase 3:**
- Selection tracking needs a companion that maps a logical range `[start_row:col, end_row:col]` to visual rows and column ranges
- Existing structure supports this (word-break indices are preserved), but no selection-mapping function yet
- Currently (line 18 comment): "Selection / highlight regions are not reflected - the body fields don't surface them visually today."

---

### 7. **OSC52 / Clipboard**

**Current status: NO clipboard or OSC52 integration exists.**

Grep results for "OSC", "52", "clipboard", "copypasta", `\x1b]52`:
- **viewer.rs line 409:** Comment explaining bracketed paste, not OSC52
- **up.rs:** References to mouse selection and Zellij's copy behavior (agent panes), not OSC52
- **No active clipboard crate** in Cargo.toml

**To implement OSC52:**
- Send escape sequence `\x1b]52;c;BASE64_CONTENT\x07` to stdout
- Requires: base64 encoding of selection text
- Scope: Only in form body; selection range must exist first
- Pattern: After user completes a selection (Shift-mouse drag or Shift-arrow), issue OSC52 on next draw or explicit keybind (e.g., Ctrl-Shift-C)

---

### 8. **Recommendation: Commit Ordering & Dependencies**

**Assessed commit sequence (smallest-first, minimum coupling):**

1. **Ctrl-U / Ctrl-D in-form paging** ✓ (smallest, most independent)
   - Modify `text_input` (todo_form.rs line 1501) to intercept Ctrl-U/Ctrl-D
   - Directly adjust `body_scroll` in todo_form (line 183) and scratchpad_form (line 110)
   - No side effects: existing viewport auto-follow code coexists
   - **Seam:** Fits cleanly before the `area.input(key)` fallback in `text_input`
   - **Self-contained:** No changes to mouse handling, render, or wrap.rs

2. **Mouse capture + click-to-position in form body** (medium)
   - Enable form routing in viewer's `handle_mouse` (currently lines 645, 651 skip forms)
   - Add `MouseEventKind::Down(MouseButton::Left)` case for form body
   - Map click position (row, col) → visual coords → logical cursor via wrap.rs (or new helper)
   - **Must come after:** Ctrl-U/D is nice-to-have; mouse requires viewer's dispatch routing to change
   - **Blocker:** None; this is a new code path

3. **Selection model + highlight + OSC52** (largest, depends on 2)
   - Add `selection: Option<(usize, usize)>` to form state (logical cursor positions)
   - Shift-mouse-drag or Shift-arrow to extend selection
   - Render: Wrap-aware mapping of logical range to visual highlights
   - OSC52: Emit on Ctrl-Shift-C or auto-on selection completion
   - **Must come after:** Mouse dispatch (2) is a prerequisite; selection is an extension
   - **Dependencies:** Requires wrap.rs companion for range mapping (new function)

**Why this order matches the code's seams:**
- **Ctrl-U/D:** Pure viewport adjustment, no dispatch changes needed
- **Mouse routing:** First change to viewer's event dispatch, enables (3)
- **Selection:** Builds on (2)'s click-to-position infra, adds new model + wrap logic

**Forced sequence:** (2) must precede (3) because selection needs mouse events to reach the form. (1) is orthogonal and can land anytime (useful on its own for keyboard-only users).

Form panes need their OWN paging gesture (Ctrl-U/Ctrl-D), their own mouse selection, and OSC52 copy.

Please read the code and report (concise, under 600 words):

1. **`crates/panopt/src/viewer.rs` shape**: It's 1736 lines. What's the top-level structure? Where is the crossterm setup (`enable_raw_mode`, `EnterAlternateScreen`, `EnableBracketedPaste`, etc.) — is `EnableMouseCapture` already on? What handles `KeyEvent` and what handles `MouseEvent`? Show the event-loop's match arms by line number.

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have enough information. Let me compile the comprehensive report:

---

## Phase 3 Planning: Form Panes UX (Mouse + OSC52 + Ctrl-U/D Paging)

### 1. **`viewer.rs` Structure (1736 lines)**

**Top-level structure:**
- Lines 1-15: Module doc
- Lines 22-38: Imports (crossterm event handling, ratatui, MCP client)
- Lines 40-45: Constants (TICK, REFRESH, DEBOUNCE durations)
- Lines 47-122: `Target` enum + helpers (parse, key, is_list, is_form)
- Lines 124-143: `Content` enum (Doc, List, Message, TodoForm, ScratchpadForm)
- Lines 145-171: `ListEntry` struct
- Lines 173-255: `TodoFilter` enum + filter logic
- Lines 256-320: `TodoSort` enum + sort logic
- Lines 434-470: `Viewer` struct
- Lines 472-1700+: impl Viewer

**Crossterm setup:**
- **Line 419**: `execute!(stdout(), EnableBracketedPaste, EnableMouseCapture)` — **both enabled at startup**
  - Bracketed paste is active and works (5eb228c added the supporting code path)
  - Mouse capture is active but only list mode routes mouse events (forms ignore them, lines 635–657)

**Event loop match arms (lines 528–536):**
```
Event::Key(key) if key.kind == KeyEventKind::Press && self.handle_key(key) => return Ok(())
Event::Paste(s) => self.handle_paste(&s)
Event::Mouse(m) => self.handle_mouse(m)
Event::Resize(_, _) => self.needs_draw = true
```

**Key handler:** `handle_key` (lines 547–613)
- Forms delegate to their own `handle_key` (TodoForm, ScratchpadForm)
- List mode: Ctrl-C, q, arrows/hjkl, PageUp/Down, Home/End, f/F (filter), s/S/d/D (sort), Enter (open)

**Mouse handler:** `handle_mouse` (lines 635–658)
- Only list mode: Left-click selects and opens a row
- ScrollUp/ScrollDown move cursor in list/doc modes
- Forms explicitly match nothing — they ignore mouse events entirely (line 656)

---

### 2. **Form Layer: Text Input & Cursor/Viewport Management**

**Both forms use `tui_textarea::TextArea` 0.7:**
- `todo_form.rs` imports: line 27: `use tui_textarea::TextArea;`
- `scratchpad_form.rs` imports: line 31: `use tui_textarea::TextArea;`

**Cursor and viewport ownership:**
- **todo_form.rs:**
  - Line 183-184: `body_scroll: usize` — manual viewport offset for body field
  - The textarea owns `cursor()` (line 1175: `self.body.cursor()`)
  - Wrapped display maps logical cursor to visual position (wrap.rs handles this)
  
- **scratchpad_form.rs:**
  - Line 110-111: `body_scroll: usize` — same manual offset
  - Same pattern: textarea owns cursor, manual wrap-aware viewport (lines 514–516)

**Vertical scrolling within body field:**
- **Already implemented:** Both forms compute `body_scroll` dynamically in `draw_body`
  - **todo_form.rs lines 1179–1189:** Cursor auto-scrolls viewport; clamped to max_scroll
  - **scratchpad_form.rs lines 518–525:** Identical logic
  - The scroll is **cursor-follow**, not independent pagination — there is no existing Ctrl-U/Ctrl-D mechanism

---

### 3. **Existing Mouse Handling**

**Grep results for MouseEvent / MouseEventKind / EnableMouseCapture / disable_mouse_capture:**

```
viewer.rs:23   [imports]
viewer.rs:24   [imports]
viewer.rs:419  execute!(stdout(), EnableBracketedPaste, EnableMouseCapture)
viewer.rs:533  Event::Mouse(m) => self.handle_mouse(m)
viewer.rs:635  fn handle_mouse(&mut self, m: MouseEvent) { ... }
viewer.rs:637    MouseEventKind::Down(MouseButton::Left) => { list row routing }
viewer.rs:644    MouseEventKind::ScrollUp => { list/doc scroll }
viewer.rs:650    MouseEventKind::ScrollDown => { list/doc scroll }
```

**Current routing:**
- `MouseCapture` is **enabled globally** at startup (line 419)
- `handle_mouse` is called for every `Event::Mouse` in the event loop (line 533)
- **List mode only:** Left-click and scroll wheel (lines 637–655)
- **Forms:** No match arms — mouse events are silently dropped inside forms

**Forms do NOT route mouse at all:** The viewer's `handle_mouse` only branches on list/doc, not forms (line 645, 651). When a form is focused, mouse events arrive but are ignored.

---

### 4. **Bracketed Paste & Ctrl Chord Forwarding (from 5eb228c)**

**Bracketed paste dispatch (lines 615–628):**
```rust
fn handle_paste(&mut self, s: &str) {
    match &mut self.content {
        Content::TodoForm(form) => {
            let _ = form.handle_paste(s);
            self.needs_draw = true;
        }
        Content::ScratchpadForm(form) => {
            let _ = form.handle_paste(s);
            self.needs_draw = true;
        }
        _ => {}
    }
}
```

**Forms' paste handling:**
- **todo_form.rs lines 353–388:** `handle_paste` routes to focused field
- **scratchpad_form.rs lines 221–236:** Same pattern
- Single-line fields use `paste_into_single_line`, multi-line use `paste_into`
- Both defined in todo_form.rs (lines 1529–1556) and re-exported

**Ctrl chord forwarding in text input (todo_form.rs lines 1501–1525):**
```rust
pub(crate) fn text_input(area: &mut TextArea, key: KeyEvent) -> bool {
    let ctrl = key.modifiers.contains(KeyModifiers::CONTROL);
    match key.code {
        KeyCode::Char('j') | KeyCode::Char('m') if ctrl => {
            area.insert_newline();  // Ctrl-J/M for newline
            true
        }
        KeyCode::Char('z') if ctrl => area.undo(),  // Ctrl-Z for undo
        _ => area.input(key),  // Everything else to textarea
    }
}
```

**Dispatch path for new Ctrl chords:**
- In `text_input` (line 1501), add branches before the fallback `area.input(key)` at line 1509
- For body field: `ScalarField::Body` branch in `scalar_input_key` (line 412) calls `text_input`
- For scratchpad body: `Field::Body` in `scratchpad_form.rs:field_key` (line 209) calls `text_input`

---

### 5. **Render Layer: Body Field Display & Scroll Offset Application**

**todo_form.rs `draw_body` (lines 1156–1205):**
```rust
fn draw_body(&mut self, frame: &mut Frame, area: Rect) {
    let cursor = self.body.cursor();
    let wrapped = crate::wrap::wrap_for_display(self.body.lines(), cursor, width);
    let (cvr, cvc) = wrapped.cursor;
    
    // Auto-adjust body_scroll to keep cursor in view
    if cvr < self.body_scroll { self.body_scroll = cvr; }
    else if cvr >= self.body_scroll + height { self.body_scroll = cvr + 1 - height; }
    let max_scroll = wrapped.lines.len().saturating_sub(height);
    if self.body_scroll > max_scroll { self.body_scroll = max_scroll; }
    
    // Render visible slice
    let visible: Vec<Line> = wrapped.lines
        .iter()
        .skip(self.body_scroll)
        .take(height)
        .map(|l| Line::from(l.clone()))
        .collect();
    frame.render_widget(Paragraph::new(visible), inner);
    
    // Overlay cursor
    if focused && cvr >= self.body_scroll && cvr < self.body_scroll + height && cvc < width {
        let cy = inner.y + (cvr - self.body_scroll) as u16;
        let cx = inner.x + cvc as u16;
        frame.set_cursor_position((cx, cy));
    }
}
```

**scratchpad_form.rs `draw_body` (lines 495–542):** Identical pattern.

**Where to apply selection highlight:**
- The visible lines (lines 1191–1197 todo_form, lines 528–534 scratchpad_form) are `Vec<Line>`
- Selection would need to map logical start/end positions through `wrap_for_display` to visual ranges
- Highlight applied in the `Span` level within each visible `Line` before render
- **Critical:** wrap.rs (lines 1–244) already maps cursor; selection would need a companion function like `wrap_for_selection(lines, cursor, start, end, width)` that returns a Vec of (row_idx, col_range) tuples

---

### 6. **wrap.rs Module (244 lines)**

**Purpose:** Soft-wrap helper for body fields.

**Key structure:**
```rust
pub(crate) struct Wrapped {
    pub lines: Vec<String>,        // Visual rows
    pub cursor: (usize, usize),    // (visual_row, visual_col)
}

pub(crate) fn wrap_for_display(
    lines: &[String],
    cursor: (usize, usize),
    width: usize
) -> Wrapped
```

**What it does:**
- **Lines 37–128:** Word-wrap with fallback to char-wrap for long tokens
- **Lines 22–30:** Logical-to-visual cursor mapping (ensures 1-to-1 char correspondence)
- **Lines 131–136:** Helper `visual_col_within` for cell-width calculation
- **Lines 18:** Uses `unicode_width::UnicodeWidthChar` for precise cell widths

**For Phase 3:**
- Selection tracking needs a companion that maps a logical range `[start_row:col, end_row:col]` to visual rows and column ranges
- Existing structure supports this (word-break indices are preserved), but no selection-mapping function yet
- Currently (line 18 comment): "Selection / highlight regions are not reflected - the body fields don't surface them visually today."

---

### 7. **OSC52 / Clipboard**

**Current status: NO clipboard or OSC52 integration exists.**

Grep results for "OSC", "52", "clipboard", "copypasta", `\x1b]52`:
- **viewer.rs line 409:** Comment explaining bracketed paste, not OSC52
- **up.rs:** References to mouse selection and Zellij's copy behavior (agent panes), not OSC52
- **No active clipboard crate** in Cargo.toml

**To implement OSC52:**
- Send escape sequence `\x1b]52;c;BASE64_CONTENT\x07` to stdout
- Requires: base64 encoding of selection text
- Scope: Only in form body; selection range must exist first
- Pattern: After user completes a selection (Shift-mouse drag or Shift-arrow), issue OSC52 on next draw or explicit keybind (e.g., Ctrl-Shift-C)

---

### 8. **Recommendation: Commit Ordering & Dependencies**

**Assessed commit sequence (smallest-first, minimum coupling):**

1. **Ctrl-U / Ctrl-D in-form paging** ✓ (smallest, most independent)
   - Modify `text_input` (todo_form.rs line 1501) to intercept Ctrl-U/Ctrl-D
   - Directly adjust `body_scroll` in todo_form (line 183) and scratchpad_form (line 110)
   - No side effects: existing viewport auto-follow code coexists
   - **Seam:** Fits cleanly before the `area.input(key)` fallback in `text_input`
   - **Self-contained:** No changes to mouse handling, render, or wrap.rs

2. **Mouse capture + click-to-position in form body** (medium)
   - Enable form routing in viewer's `handle_mouse` (currently lines 645, 651 skip forms)
   - Add `MouseEventKind::Down(MouseButton::Left)` case for form body
   - Map click position (row, col) → visual coords → logical cursor via wrap.rs (or new helper)
   - **Must come after:** Ctrl-U/D is nice-to-have; mouse requires viewer's dispatch routing to change
   - **Blocker:** None; this is a new code path

3. **Selection model + highlight + OSC52** (largest, depends on 2)
   - Add `selection: Option<(usize, usize)>` to form state (logical cursor positions)
   - Shift-mouse-drag or Shift-arrow to extend selection
   - Render: Wrap-aware mapping of logical range to visual highlights
   - OSC52: Emit on Ctrl-Shift-C or auto-on selection completion
   - **Must come after:** Mouse dispatch (2) is a prerequisite; selection is an extension
   - **Dependencies:** Requires wrap.rs companion for range mapping (new function)

**Why this order matches the code's seams:**
- **Ctrl-U/D:** Pure viewport adjustment, no dispatch changes needed
- **Mouse routing:** First change to viewer's event dispatch, enables (3)
- **Selection:** Builds on (2)'s click-to-position infra, adds new model + wrap logic

**Forced sequence:** (2) must precede (3) because selection needs mouse events to reach the form. (1) is orthogonal and can land anytime (useful on its own for keyboard-only users).

+/// `Ctrl-D` is the convention every vim/less user already has in muscle
+/// Page step is `(view_height / 2).max(1)`. A press before the first draw
+/// (when `view_height` is still zero) moves one row, so the gesture is

+/// follow-logic then catches up to keep it on screen.

git add crates/panopt/src/todo_form.rs crates/panopt/src/scratchpad_form.rs && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Phase 3a of #95: Ctrl-U / Ctrl-D page form bodies by half a screen

Phase 2 globally bound PageUp / PageDown to Zellij's PageScrollUp /
PageScrollDown, which means a focused form pane (`panopt _viewer` on
the terminal's alt-screen) sees those keys do nothing - there's no
Zellij scrollback to scroll. The form needs its own paging gesture.

`body_input` wraps `text_input` with two new intercepts: Ctrl-D pages
the cursor down by half the visible body height, Ctrl-U pages up. The
existing viewport-follow logic in `draw_body` then catches up to keep
the cursor on screen. Half-page is the vim / less convention every
likely user already has in muscle memory.

The Body field's row count is captured into a new `body_view_height`
on each draw, so the step adapts to pane resizes. A press before the
first draw falls back to a one-row step so the gesture is never inert.

Both forms share the helper; `scratchpad_form` imports `body_input`
from `todo_form` (where the textarea-input helpers already live).

The textarea's stock Ctrl-U (undo) and Ctrl-D (delete-next-char) are
shadowed in the body field; Ctrl-Z still does undo (the existing
override) and Delete still forward-deletes. Single-line fields keep
the textarea defaults.

Three unit tests: half-page paging both directions, the one-row
fallback before the first draw, and a regression that ordinary typing
still flows through to the textarea.
EOF
)" && git log --oneline -3

git diff --stat && echo "---" && git add crates/panopt/src/wrap.rs crates/panopt/src/todo_form.rs crates/panopt/src/scratchpad_form.rs crates/panopt/src/viewer.rs && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Phase 3b of #95: left-click positions the body field's cursor

Mouse capture is already enabled at startup (viewer.rs:419), but the
viewer's `handle_mouse` ignored every event when a form was focused.
Route left-clicks into a new `Form::handle_mouse` so click-to-position
in the body field works for both the todo and scratchpad viewers.

- `wrap::Wrapped` gains a `segments: Vec<(usize, usize)>` field
  pairing each visual row with `(logical_row, char_start)`, plus a
  `visual_to_logical(vrow, vcol)` method that walks the segment's
  chars by cell width to find the matching logical column. Inverse of
  the existing forward map, kept consistent by being produced in the
  same wrap pass.
- `TodoForm` / `ScratchpadForm` cache the body's screen rectangle on
  every render (`body_area: Option<Rect>`); `handle_mouse` reads it to
  decide whether the click hit the body, then converts the click into
  visual coords (adding `body_scroll`) and on to a logical cursor via
  the new map. Focus snaps to Body so the next keystroke edits where
  the user just clicked.
- `Viewer::handle_mouse` forwards every mouse event to the focused
  form when one is up; list/doc routing is preserved unchanged in the
  fallback arm. The form returns `Idle` (no content edit) and
  `needs_draw` is set unconditionally so the new cursor renders next
  frame.

Five new tests cover: the visual-to-logical inverse on a wrapped
segment, past-end clamping, an empty logical line, the click-positions
path end-to-end (text "hello world", click at column 6, cursor lands
at col 6), and an out-of-rect click being a no-op.

Drag-to-select, OSC52 copy, and mouse-wheel routing inside the form
are reserved for Phase 3c.
EOF
)" && git log --oneline -5

git diff --stat && echo "---" && git add crates/panopt/src/clip.rs crates/panopt/src/main.rs crates/panopt/src/wrap.rs crates/panopt/src/todo_form.rs crates/panopt/src/scratchpad_form.rs && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Phase 3c of #95: drag-to-select in form bodies, OSC 52 copy

The form pane's body field grows the rest of the regular-terminal UX:
mouse drag selects, the selection renders in reverse video, and a
release-after-drag puts the selected text in the system clipboard via
the OSC 52 escape sequence. Ctrl-Shift-C re-copies the same selection
without releasing.

- `crate::clip`: new module with a hand-rolled base64 encoder and
  `emit_osc52(text)`. Writes `\x1b]52;c;<base64>\x07` straight to
  stdout; the viewer holds the terminal in raw alt-screen mode, so
  the bytes pass through Zellij to the host terminal which writes
  them into the system clipboard. No crate dep, no daemon (no xclip,
  no wl-copy). Two unit tests (RFC 4648 vectors + envelope shape).
- `wrap::Wrapped::visual_selection_ranges`: companion to
  `visual_to_logical` from Phase 3b - given a logical `(anchor, tip)`
  pair, returns the per-visual-row char ranges to highlight. Normalizes
  backward drags. Three new tests.
- `todo_form::selected_text`: extracts the substring under a selection
  from `lines()`, joining multi-line ranges with `\n` (what a paste-back
  would expect). Three unit tests.
- `todo_form::highlight_line`: builds a styled `Line` from a visual row
  + char range using `Modifier::REVERSED`, the universal "selected"
  cue across terminal emulators.
- `TodoForm` / `ScratchpadForm` gain a `selection: Option<(anchor, tip)>`
  and rewire `handle_mouse` to the Down / Drag / Up cycle: Down anchors,
  Drag extends (the cursor follows the tip so the user sees where they
  are), Up either emits OSC 52 or clears a zero-length click selection.
  Scroll wheel walks the textarea cursor by one row.
- `handle_key` intercepts `Ctrl-Shift-C` ahead of the existing Ctrl-C
  close arm, re-emitting OSC 52 for the current selection. Every other
  keystroke clears the selection so the highlight does not linger over
  text the user is editing past.
- `draw_body` queries `visual_selection_ranges` and renders each visible
  visual row through `highlight_line` when the range intersects it; rows
  without a selection use the existing plain `Line::from(l.clone())`.

The single existing pre-existing test failure (`mcp::tests::
agent_mcp_json_is_valid_stdio_config`) is unchanged by this commit -
it depends on the runtime `PANOPT_BIN` env, not on anything Phase 3
touched.
EOF
)" && git log --oneline -8

content.text:
Phase 3 of #95 is in three commits on `right-pane-terminal-ux-phase1`:

- **`0dc3d5b` Phase 3a** — Ctrl-U/D page form bodies by half a screen (vim/less convention)
- **`5b7df64` Phase 3b** — left-click positions cursor in body via wrap-aware visual→logical inverse
- **`2baf984` Phase 3c** — drag-to-select, reverse-video highlight, OSC 52 copy on release, Ctrl-Shift-C re-copies

To exercise: `just refresh && panopt up`, open a todo, focus Body, then:
1. Long press a few enters to make scrollable content; Ctrl-D / Ctrl-U should page by half-screen.
2. Left-click somewhere mid-text — cursor jumps there.
3. Drag across a span; reverse-video highlight should track; release puts the span in the system clipboard. Ctrl-Shift-C re-copies the same span.

Heads up:
- I left `wrap`'s `visual_to_logical` and `visual_selection_ranges` agnostic to double-width chars in the *click target* sense (clicking on a wide char's right half maps to that char) — fine for ASCII, may need tweaking if you live-test on CJK.
- `mcp::tests::agent_mcp_json_is_valid_stdio_config` is a pre-existing failure dependent on `PANOPT_BIN` env state, not introduced by Phase 3.
- I did NOT runtime-test the cockpit — all three pieces have unit tests but the OSC 52 emit, mouse drag wiring, and reverse-video rendering only meet reality when you exercise them live.

Three todos (#93, #94, #95) still `in_progress`. Mark them when ready, or `mac #93 #94 #95` and I'll close all three.
